{{Quote|Luke... save the world for me, okay?|Mary|<ref name="SW89" />}}
'''Solay''' was the main planet in the [[Solay system/Legends|Solay system]] in the [[Dominus sector/Legends|Dominus sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Home to a variety of [[Species/Legends|species]], Solay remained in the hands of a despot for half a [[Standard century/Legends|century]], before the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] assisted the resisters. The rebel victory was short-lived, however, as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] quickly annexed Solay, forcing Skywalker to flee.

==Description==
The [[Planet/Legends|world]] of Solay was the main planet of an [[Solay system/Legends|eponymous]] [[Star system/Legends|star system]], a rare case of decuple system—with ten [[Star/Legends|suns]] revolving around a common center of mass.<ref name="SW89" />

==History==
{{Quote|Some Imperial governor used the rebels to do his dirty work. He found one rebel who had a price... and set everyone up for a fall. Now Solay is ''his''..|[[Scamp]]|<ref name="SW89" />}}
From around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[46 BBY/Legends|46 BBY]], Solay was ruled by a local tyrant, [[King/Legends|King]] [[Blackart]]. Following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Blackart ruled his planet as the vassal of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], after half a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] of fighting, the local resistance against King Blackart's tyranny won over his forces.<ref name="SW89" />

The resistance, led by [[Raggold]] and [[Mary]], enjoyed the support of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in training and hero of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] that opposed Palpatine and his Empire. While the the resisters prepared for the last battle, an unexpected turn of events occurred. Raggold had, unbeknownst to all, betrayed the planet to the Empire, promising to use Imperial support to defeat Blackart and thus allow them direct control over the planet. Consumed by the guilt over his betrayal and what it would lead to now that he had won the war, Raggold committed [[Death/Legends|suicide]]. The Empire shortly thereafter resubjugated the world as planned, killing Mary in the process. Being unable to help, Skywalker escaped the planet with the help of a local [[Information broker/Legends|infochant]] named [[Braxas]].<ref name="SW89" />

==Behind the scenes==
Solay was the setting of [[Star Wars (1977) 89|''Star Wars'' 89]], written by [[Ann Nocenti]], illustrated by [[Bret Blevins]] and released in [[1984]].<ref name="SW89" />
